turns: Hello, is this the number for the Trial By Jury program?
Yes, it is. How can I help you today?
I'm interested in participating in the program as a juror. Can you tell me more about it?
Sure, Trial By Jury is a program that selects random members of the community to serve as jurors in real criminal trials.
That sounds really interesting. How does the selection process work?

We use a random selection process to choose potential jurors from a pool of registered voters. If you're selected, you'll receive a summons in the mail with the details of the trial you'll be participating in.
That sounds great. How often do you have trials?
We have trials fairly regularly, so it's possible you could be selected to serve as a juror at any time.
And how long does the trial usually last?
The length of the trial can vary, but most criminal trials last anywhere from a few days to a couple of weeks.
And what kind of criminal cases are tried in this program?
We try a wide range of criminal cases, including murder, robbery, and fraud cases.
That sounds like a really important and interesting program. I'd be happy to participate if I'm selected.
That's great to hear. We're always in need of willing participants to ensure a fair and impartial justice system.
What are the requirements to participate in the program?
To participate in the program, you must be a registered voter and a resident of the county where the trial is taking place.
And what are the chances of being selected to serve as a juror?
The chances of being selected depend on a variety of factors, including the number of potential jurors in the pool and the specific case being tried.
And what kind of training do the jurors receive before the trial?
We provide training to all potential jurors to ensure they understand the legal system and their role in it.
That sounds great, thank you for explaining everything to me.
If you have any further questions, feel free to give us a call.
Do I need to do anything to register for the program?
No, you're automatically registered when you receive your summons in the mail.
Okay, thanks. I'll be on the lookout for the summons then.
Of course, if you have any questions about the trial process or your role as a juror, don't hesitate to contact us. We're here to help.
Okay, thank you for your help. I'll be on the lookout for the summons then.
You're welcome, have a great day!
